seoTitle: Stone Hut vs Stone Well — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Stone Hut and Stone Well. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Stone Hut vs Stone Well: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stone Hut and Stone Well.
heading: Stone Hut vs Stone Well — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stone Hut, South Australia and Stone Well, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stone Hut has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Stone Well (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stone Hut or Stone Well safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stone Hut has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Stone Well scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stone Hut scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Stone Well scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stone Hut or Stone Well?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stone Hut has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
